NKJV.1: To the Chief Musician. With flutes. A Psalm of David. Give ear to my words, O LORD, Consider my meditation.
NKJV.2: Give heed to the voice of my cry, My King and my God, For to You I will pray.
NKJV.3: My voice You shall hear in the morning, O LORD; In the morning I will direct it to You, And I will look up.
NKJV.4: For You are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness, Nor shall evil dwell with You.
NKJV.5: The boastful shall not stand in Your sight; You hate all workers of iniquity.
NKJV.6: You shall destroy those who speak falsehood; The LORD abhors the bloodthirsty and deceitful man.
NKJV.7: But as for me, I will come into Your house in the multitude of Your mercy; In fear of You I will worship toward Your holy temple.
NKJV.8: Lead me, O LORD, in Your righteousness because of my enemies; Make Your way straight before my face.
NKJV.9: For there is no faithfulness in their mouth; Their inward part is destruction; Their throat is an open tomb; They flatter with their tongue.
NKJV.10: Pronounce them guilty, O God! Let them fall by their own counsels; Cast them out in the multitude of their transgressions, For they have rebelled against You.
NKJV.11: But let all those rejoice who put their trust in You; Let them ever shout for joy, because You defend them; Let those also who love Your name Be joyful in You.
NKJV.12: For You, O LORD, will bless the righteous; With favor You will surround him as with a shield.

KJV.1: To the chief Musician<S>5329</S> upon Nehiloth<S>5155</S>, A Psalm<S>4210</S> of David<S>1732</S>. Give ear<S>238</S> to my words<S>561</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>, consider<S>995</S> my meditation<S>1901</S>.
KJV.2: Hearken<S>7181</S> unto the voice<S>6963</S> of my cry<S>7773</S>, my King<S>4428</S>, and my God<S>430</S>: for unto thee will I pray<S>6419</S>.
KJV.3: My voice<S>6963</S> shalt thou hear<S>8085</S> in the morning<S>1242</S>, O LORD<S>3068</S>; in the morning<S>1242</S> will I direct<S>6186</S> my prayer unto thee, and will look up<S>6822</S>.
KJV.4: For thou art not a God<S>410</S> that hath pleasure<S>2655</S> in wickedness<S>7562</S>: neither shall evil<S>7451</S> dwell<S>1481</S> with thee.
KJV.5: The foolish<S>1984</S> shall not stand<S>3320</S> in thy sight<S>5869</S>: thou hatest<S>8130</S> all workers<S>6466</S> of iniquity<S>205</S>. <sup>in: Heb. before thine eyes</sup>
KJV.6: Thou shalt destroy<S>6</S> them that speak<S>1696</S> leasing<S>3577</S>: the LORD<S>3068</S> will abhor<S>8581</S> the bloody<S>1818</S> and deceitful<S>4820</S> man<S>376</S>. <sup>bloody: Heb. man of bloods and deceit</sup>
KJV.7: But as for me, I will come<S>935</S> into thy house<S>1004</S> in the multitude<S>7230</S> of thy mercy<S>2617</S>: and in thy fear<S>3374</S> will I worship<S>7812</S> toward thy holy<S>6944</S> temple<S>1964</S>. <sup>thy holy: Heb. the temple of thy holiness</sup>
KJV.8: Lead<S>5148</S> me, O LORD<S>3068</S>, in thy righteousness<S>6666</S> because of mine enemies<S>8324</S>; make thy way<S>1870</S> straight<S>3474</S> before my face<S>6440</S>. <sup>mine: Heb. those which observe me</sup>
KJV.9: For there is no faithfulness<S>3559</S> in their mouth<S>6310</S>; their inward<S>7130</S> part is very wickedness<S>1942</S>; their throat<S>1627</S> is an open<S>6605</S> sepulchre<S>6913</S>; they flatter<S>2505</S> with their tongue<S>3956</S>. <sup>faithfulness: or, stedfastness</sup> <sup>their mouth: Heb. his mouth, that is, the mouth of any of them</sup> <sup>very: Heb. wickednesses</sup>
KJV.10: Destroy<S>816</S> thou them, O God<S>430</S>; let them fall<S>5307</S> by their own counsels<S>4156</S>; cast them out<S>5080</S> in the multitude<S>7230</S> of their transgressions<S>6588</S>; for they have rebelled<S>4784</S> against thee. <sup>Destroy: or, Make them guilty</sup> <sup>by: or, from their counsels</sup>
KJV.11: But let all those that put their trust<S>2620</S> in thee rejoice<S>8055</S>: let them ever<S>5769</S> shout for joy<S>7442</S>, because thou defendest<S>5526</S> them: let them also that love<S>157</S> thy name<S>8034</S> be joyful<S>5970</S> in thee. <sup>defendest: Heb. coverest over, or, protectest them</sup>
KJV.12: For thou, LORD<S>3068</S>, wilt bless<S>1288</S> the righteous<S>6662</S>; with favour<S>7522</S> wilt thou compass<S>5849</S> him as with a shield<S>6793</S>. <sup>compass: Heb. crown</sup>

ESV.1: Give ear to my words, O LORD; consider my groaning.
ESV.2: Give attention to the sound of my cry, my King and my God, for to you do I pray.
ESV.3: O LORD, in the morning you hear my voice; in the morning I prepare a sacrifice for you  and watch.
ESV.4: For you are not a God who delights in wickedness; evil may not dwell with you.
ESV.5: The boastful shall not stand before your eyes; you hate all evildoers.
ESV.6: You destroy those who speak lies; the LORD abhors the bloodthirsty and deceitful man.
ESV.7: But I, through the abundance of your steadfast love, will enter your house. I will bow down toward your holy temple in the fear of you.
ESV.8: Lead me, O LORD, in your righteousness because of my enemies; make your way straight before me.
ESV.9: For there is no truth in their mouth; their inmost self is destruction; their throat is an open grave; they flatter with their tongue.
ESV.10: Make them bear their guilt, O God; let them fall by their own counsels; because of the abundance of their transgressions cast them out, for they have rebelled against you.
ESV.11: But let all who take refuge in you rejoice; let them ever sing for joy, and spread your protection over them, that those who love your name may exult in you.
ESV.12: For you bless the righteous, O LORD; you cover him with favor as with a shield.

NASB.1: For the choir director; for flute accompaniment. A Psalm of David. Give ear to my words, O LORD, Consider my groaning.
NASB.2: Heed the sound of my cry for help, my King and my God, For to You I pray.
NASB.3: In the morning, O LORD, You will hear my voice; In the morning I will order [my] [prayer] to You and [eagerly] watch.
NASB.4: For You are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness; No evil dwells with You.
NASB.5: The boastful shall not stand before Your eyes; You hate all who do iniquity.
NASB.6: You destroy those who speak falsehood; The LORD abhors the man of bloodshed and deceit.
NASB.7: But as for me, by Your abundant lovingkindness I will enter Your house, At Your holy temple I will bow in reverence for You.
NASB.8: O LORD, lead me in Your righteousness because of my foes; Make Your way straight before me.
NASB.9: There is nothing reliable in what they say; Their inward part is destruction [itself]. Their throat is an open grave; They flatter with their tongue.
NASB.10: Hold them guilty, O God; By their own devices let them fall! In the multitude of their transgressions thrust them out, For they are rebellious against You.
NASB.11: But let all who take refuge in You be glad, Let them ever sing for joy; And may You shelter them, That those who love Your name may exult in You.
NASB.12: For it is You who blesses the righteous man, O LORD, You surround him with favor as with a shield.

NLT.1:
O LORD, hear me as I pray;
pay attention to my groaning.
NLT.2:
Listen to my cry for help, my King and my God,
for I pray to no one but you.
NLT.3:
Listen to my voice in the morning, LORD.
Each morning I bring my requests to you and wait expectantly.
NLT.4:
O God, you take no pleasure in wickedness;
you cannot tolerate the sins of the wicked.
NLT.5:
Therefore, the proud may not stand in your presence,
for you hate all who do evil.
NLT.6:
You will destroy those who tell lies.
The LORD detests murderers and deceivers.
NLT.7:
Because of your unfailing love, I can enter your house;
I will worship at your Temple with deepest awe.
NLT.8:
Lead me in the right path, O LORD,
or my enemies will conquer me.
Make your way plain for me to follow.
NLT.9:
My enemies cannot speak a truthful word.
Their deepest desire is to destroy others.
Their talk is foul, like the stench from an open grave.
Their tongues are filled with flattery.
NLT.10:
O God, declare them guilty.
Let them be caught in their own traps.
Drive them away because of their many sins,
for they have rebelled against you.
NLT.11:
But let all who take refuge in you rejoice;
let them sing joyful praises forever.
Spread your protection over them,
that all who love your name may be filled with joy.
NLT.12:
For you bless the godly, O LORD;
you surround them with your shield of love.

WEB.1: For the Chief Musician, with the flutes. A Psalm by David. Give ear to my words, Yahweh. Consider my meditation.
WEB.2: Listen to the voice of my cry, my King and my God; for to you do I pray.
WEB.3: Yahweh, in the morning you shall hear my voice. In the morning I will lay my requests before you, and will watch expectantly.
WEB.4: For you are not a God who has pleasure in wickedness. Evil can’t live with you.
WEB.5: The arrogant shall not stand in your sight. You hate all workers of iniquity.
WEB.6: You will destroy those who speak lies. Yahweh abhors the bloodthirsty and deceitful man.
WEB.7: But as for me, in the abundance of your loving kindness I will come into your house. I will bow toward your holy temple in reverence of you.
WEB.8: Lead me, Yahweh, in your righteousness because of my enemies. Make your way straight before my face.
WEB.9: For there is no faithfulness in their mouth. Their heart is destruction. Their throat is an open tomb. They flatter with their tongue.
WEB.10: Hold them guilty, God. Let them fall by their own counsels; Thrust them out in the multitude of their transgressions, for they have rebelled against you.
WEB.11: But let all those who take refuge in you rejoice, Let them always shout for joy, because you defend them. Let them also who love your name be joyful in you.
WEB.12: For you will bless the righteous. Yahweh, you will surround him with favor as with a shield.

consider my sighing. ⓚ

by the abundance of your faithful love; ⓖ

AMP.1:
To the Chief Musician; on wind instruments. A Psalm of David. Listen to my words, O LORD,
Consider my groaning and sighing.
AMP.2:
Heed the sound of my cry for help, my King and my God,
For to You I pray.
AMP.3:
In the morning, O LORD, You will hear my voice;
In the morning I will prepare [a prayer and a sacrifice] for You and watch and wait [for You to speak to my heart].
AMP.4:
For You are not a God who takes pleasure in wickedness;
No evil [person] dwells with You.
AMP.5:
The boastful and the arrogant will not stand in Your sight;
You hate all who do evil.
AMP.6:
You destroy those who tell lies;
The LORD detests and rejects the bloodthirsty and deceitful man.
AMP.7:
But as for me, I will enter Your house through the abundance of Your steadfast love and tender mercy;
At Your holy temple I will bow [obediently] in reverence for You.
AMP.8:
O LORD, lead me in Your righteousness because of my enemies;
Make Your way straight (direct, right) before me.
AMP.9:
For there is nothing trustworthy or reliable or truthful in what they say;
Their heart is destruction [just a treacherous chasm, a yawning gulf of lies].
Their throat is an open grave;
They [glibly] flatter with their [silken] tongue.
AMP.10:
Hold them guilty, O God;
Let them fall by their own designs and councils!
Cast them out because of the abundance of their transgressions,
For they are mutinous and have rebelled against You.
AMP.11:
But let all who take refuge and put their trust in You rejoice,
Let them ever sing for joy;
Because You cover and shelter them,
Let those who love Your name be joyful and exult in You.
AMP.12:
For You, O LORD, bless the righteous man [the one who is in right standing with You];
You surround him with favor as with a shield.

MSG.1:
A David psalm
Listen, God! Please, pay attention!
Can you make sense of these ramblings,
MSG.2:
my groans and cries?
King-God, I need your help.
MSG.3:
Every morning
you'll hear me at it again.
Every morning
I lay out the pieces of my life
on your altar
and watch for fire to descend.
MSG.4:
You don't socialize with Wicked,
or invite Evil over as your houseguest.
MSG.5:
Hot-Air-Boaster collapses in front of you;
you shake your head over Mischief-Maker.
MSG.6:
God destroys Lie-Speaker;
Blood-Thirsty and Truth-Bender disgust you.
MSG.7:
And here I am, your invited guest—
it's incredible!
I enter your house; here I am,
prostrate in your inner sanctum,
MSG.8:
Waiting for directions
to get me safely through enemy lines.
MSG.9:
Every word they speak is a land mine;
their lungs breathe out poison gas.
Their throats are gaping graves,
their tongues slick as mudslides.
MSG.10:
Pile on the guilt, God!
Let their so-called wisdom wreck them.
Kick them out! They've had their chance.
MSG.11:
But you'll welcome us with open arms
when we run for cover to you.
Let the party last all night!
Stand guard over our celebration.
MSG.12:
You are famous, God, for welcoming God-seekers,
for decking us out in delight.

NIV.1:
Psalm 5
For the director of music. For flutes. A psalm of David.
Give ear to my words, O Lord,
consider my sighing.
NIV.2:
Listen to my cry for help,
my King and my God,
for to you I pray.
NIV.3:
In the morning, O Lord, you hear my voice;
in the morning I lay my requests before you
and wait in expectation.
NIV.4:
You are not a God who takes pleasure in evil;
with you the wicked cannot dwell.
NIV.5:
The arrogant cannot stand in your presence;
you hate all who do wrong.
NIV.6:
You destroy those who tell lies;
bloodthirsty and deceitful men
the Lord abhors.
NIV.7:
But I, by your great mercy,
will come into your house;
in reverence will I bow down
toward your holy temple.
NIV.8:
Lead me, O Lord, in your righteousness
because of my enemies—
make straight your way before me.
NIV.9:
Not a word from their mouth can be trusted;
their heart is filled with destruction.
Their throat is an open grave;
with their tongue they speak deceit.
NIV.10:
Declare them guilty, O God!
Let their intrigues be their downfall.
Banish them for their many sins,
for they have rebelled against you.
NIV.11:
But let all who take refuge in you be glad;
let them ever sing for joy.
Spread your protection over them,
that those who love your name may rejoice in you.
NIV.12:
For surely, O Lord, you bless the righteous;
you surround them with your favor as with a shield.

NRSVCE.1:
Give ear to my words, O LORD;
give heed to my sighing.
NRSVCE.2:
Listen to the sound of my cry,
my King and my God,
for to you I pray.
NRSVCE.3:
O LORD, in the morning you hear my voice;
in the morning I plead my case to you, and watch.
NRSVCE.4:
For you are not a God who delights in wickedness;
evil will not sojourn with you.
NRSVCE.5:
The boastful will not stand before your eyes;
you hate all evildoers.
NRSVCE.6:
You destroy those who speak lies;
the LORD abhors the bloodthirsty and deceitful.
NRSVCE.7:
But I, through the abundance of your steadfast love,
will enter your house,
I will bow down toward your holy temple
in awe of you.
NRSVCE.8:
Lead me, O LORD, in your righteousness
because of my enemies;
make your way straight before me.
NRSVCE.9:
For there is no truth in their mouths;
their hearts are destruction;
their throats are open graves;
they flatter with their tongues.
NRSVCE.10:
Make them bear their guilt, O God;
let them fall by their own counsels;
because of their many transgressions cast them out,
for they have rebelled against you.
NRSVCE.11:
But let all who take refuge in you rejoice;
let them ever sing for joy.
Spread your protection over them,
so that those who love your name may exult in you.
NRSVCE.12:
For you bless the righteous, O LORD;
you cover them with favor as with a shield.

NET.1: Listen to what I say, LORD! Carefully consider my complaint!
NET.2: Pay attention to my cry for help, my king and my God, for I am praying to you!
NET.3: LORD, in the morning you will hear me; in the morning I will present my case to you and then wait expectantly for an answer.
NET.4: Certainly you are not a God who approves of evil; evil people cannot dwell with you.
NET.5: Arrogant people cannot stand in your presence; you hate all who behave wickedly.
NET.6: You destroy liars; the LORD despises violent and deceitful people.
NET.7: But as for me, because of your great faithfulness I will enter your house; I will bow down toward your holy temple as I worship you.
NET.8: LORD, lead me in your righteousness because of those who wait to ambush me, remove the obstacles in the way in which you are guiding me!
NET.9: For they do not speak the truth; their stomachs are like the place of destruction, their throats like an open grave, their tongues like a steep slope leading into it.
NET.10: Condemn them, O God! May their own schemes be their downfall! Drive them away because of their many acts of insurrection, for they have rebelled against you.
NET.11: But may all who take shelter in you be happy! May they continually shout for joy! Shelter them so that those who are loyal to you may rejoice!
NET.12: Certainly you reward the godly, LORD. Like a shield you protect them in your good favor.
